001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.markdown;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.io.*;
023import java.util.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
033
034/**
035 * Session object for {@link MarkdownParser}.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Parses Markdown tables and bulleted lists into Java objects.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
042 * </ul>
043 *
044 */
045@SuppressWarnings({
046   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for parser session hierarchy
047   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
048   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for doParse / parseAnything
049   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for parseAnything
050   "unchecked",
051   "rawtypes",
052   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
053})
054public class MarkdownParserSession extends ReaderParserSession implements RecordReadable {
055
056   private static final String CONST_type = "_type";
057
058   final String nullValue;
059   private final Memoizer<JsonParser> json5Parser = memoizer(() -> {
060      var b = Json5Parser.create().marshallingContext((MarshallingContext) getContext());
061      if (isTrimStrings())
062         b = b.trimStrings();
063      return b.build();
064   });
065
066   /**
067    * Builder class.
068    */
069   @SuppressWarnings({
070      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
071   })
072   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends ReaderParserSession.Builder<SELF> {
073
074      String nullValue;
075
076      /**
077       * Constructor.
078       *
079       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
080       */
081      protected Builder(MarkdownParser ctx) {
082         super(ctx);
083         nullValue = ctx.getNullValue();
084      }
085
086      @Override
087      public MarkdownParserSession build() {
088         return new MarkdownParserSession(this);
089      }
090
091   }
092
093   /**
094    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
095    */
096   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
097
098      DefaultBuilder(MarkdownParser ctx) {
099         super(ctx);
100      }
101   }
102
103   /**
104    * Creates a new builder for this object.
105    *
106    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
107    * @return A new builder.
108    */
109   @SuppressWarnings({
110      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
111   })
112   public static Builder<?> create(MarkdownParser ctx) {
113      return new DefaultBuilder(ctx);
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Constructor.
118    *
119    * @param builder The builder for this object.
120    */
121   protected MarkdownParserSession(Builder<?> builder) {
122      super(builder);
123      nullValue = builder.nullValue != null ? builder.nullValue : "*null*";
124   }
125
126   @Override /* RecordReadable */
127   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
128      return RecordAdapter.reader(this, input);
129   }
130
131   @Override /* RecordReadable */
132   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
133      return false;
134   }
135
136   @Override /* Overridden from ParserSession */
137   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
138      try (var r = new BufferedReader(pipe.getReader())) {
139         var lines = readAllLines(r);
140         return parseAnything(lines, type, getOuter(), null);
141      }
142   }
143
144   /**
145    * Main parse dispatch method.
146    *
147    * @param <T> The target type.
148    * @param lines All input lines.
149    * @param eType The expected type.
150    * @param outer The outer object (for inner class instantiation).
151    * @param pMeta The parent bean property meta (may be null).
152    * @return The parsed object.
153    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
154    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
155    */
156   protected <T> T parseAnything(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<T> eType, Object outer, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, ParseException {
157      if (eType == null)
158         eType = (ClassMeta<T>) object();
159
160      var swap = (ObjectSwap<T,Object>) eType.getSwap(this);
161      var builder = (BuilderSwap<T,Object>) eType.getBuilderSwap(this);
162      ClassMeta<?> sType;
163      if (builder != null)
164         sType = builder.getBuilderClassMeta(this);
165      else if (swap != null)
166         sType = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
167      else
168         sType = eType;
169
170      if (sType.isOptional())
171         return (T) o(parseAnything(lines, eType.getElementType(), outer, pMeta));
172
173      // Skip blank lines and handle empty input
174      var nonBlank = lines.stream().filter(l -> !l.isBlank()).toList();
175      if (lines.isEmpty() || nonBlank.isEmpty())
176         return sType.isString() ? (T) "" : null;
177
178      var firstNonBlank = nonBlank.get(0).trim();
179
180      Object o;
181
182      if (firstNonBlank.startsWith("|")) {
183         // Table input
184         o = parseTable(lines, sType, outer);
185      } else if (firstNonBlank.startsWith("- ") || firstNonBlank.startsWith("* ") || firstNonBlank.startsWith("+ ")) {
186         // Bulleted list input
187         o = parseBulletList(lines, sType, outer);
188      } else {
189         // Plain text — treat as simple value
190         o = parseCellValue(firstNonBlank, sType, null);
191      }
192
193      if (builder != null && o != null)
194         o = builder.build(this, o, eType);
195
196      if (swap != null && o != null)
197         o = unswap(swap, o, eType);
198
199      return (T) o;
200   }
201
202   /**
203    * Parses a Markdown table (either 2-column key/value or multi-column).
204    *
205    * @param lines All input lines.
206    * @param eType The expected type.
207    * @param outer The outer object.
208    * @return The parsed object.
209    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
210    */
211   protected Object parseTable(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
212      // Extract table rows (lines starting with |)
213      var tableLines = lines.stream().filter(l -> l.trim().startsWith("|")).toList();
214      if (tableLines.isEmpty())
215         return null;
216
217      // Parse header row
218      var headerLine = tableLines.get(0);
219      var headers = splitTableRow(headerLine);
220      if (headers.isEmpty())
221         return null;
222
223      // Skip separator row (|---|---|)
224      var dataLines = tableLines.stream().skip(1).filter(l -> !isSeparatorRow(l)).toList();
225
226      // Detect table type: if 2 columns and first header is "Property"/"Key", treat as key/value table
227      var isKeyValue = headers.size() == 2
228         && (headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Property") || headers.get(0).equalsIgnoreCase("Key"))
229         && headers.get(1).equalsIgnoreCase("Value");
230
231      if (isKeyValue)
232         return parseKeyValueTable(headers, dataLines, eType, outer);
233      return parseMultiColumnTable(headers, dataLines, eType, outer);
234   }
235
236   /**
237    * Parses a 2-column key/value Markdown table.
238    *
239    * @param headers The header names.
240    * @param dataLines The data row lines.
241    * @param eType The expected type.
242    * @param outer The outer object.
243    * @return The parsed bean, map, or other object.
244    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
245    */
246   @SuppressWarnings({
247      "java:S135" // Two continue statements needed: skip short rows and skip _type row
248   })
249   protected Object parseKeyValueTable(List<String> headers, List<String> dataLines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
250
251      // For types with ObjectSwaps or that can't be populated via BeanMap property-setting,
252      // convert the key-value table to a JSON5 object and delegate to the full JSON5 parser.
253      if (needsJson5Path(eType)) {
254         var json5 = keyValueTableToJson5(dataLines);
255         try {
256            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(json5, eType, outer);
257         } catch (Exception e) {
258            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse key-value table as ''{0}'' via JSON5 ''{1}''.", eType, json5);
259         }
260      }
261
262      if (eType.isBean()) {
263         // First pass: look for _type to resolve concrete class for abstract/interface types
264         ClassMeta<?> actualType = eType;
265         for (var line : dataLines) {
266            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
267            if (cells.size() >= 2 && CONST_type.equals(cells.get(0))) {
268               var typeName = cells.get(1);
269               var registry = eType.getBeanRegistry();
270               var resolved = registry != null ? registry.getClassMeta(typeName) : null;
271               if (resolved != null)
272                  actualType = resolved;
273               break;
274            }
275         }
276         var m = newBeanMap(outer, actualType.inner());
277         for (var line : dataLines) {
278            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
279            if (cells.size() < 2)
280               continue;
281            var key = cells.get(0);
282
283            // Skip _type row - it was used for type resolution above
284            if (CONST_type.equals(key))
285               continue;
286
287            var rawVal = cells.get(1);
288            var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(key);
289            if (pm != null) {
290               setCurrentProperty(pm);
291               var pmcm = (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo();
292               var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, pmcm, m.getBean(false));
293               try {
294                  setName(pmcm, val, key);
295               } catch (Exception e) {
296                  throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not set @NameProperty on ''{0}''.", key);
297               }
298               pm.set(m, key, val);
299               setCurrentProperty(null);
300            } else {
301               onUnknownProperty(key, m, rawVal);
302            }
303         }
304         return m.getBean();
305      }
306
307      if (eType.isMap()) {
308         @SuppressWarnings({
309            "java:S3740" // Raw Map needed for generic map construction from ClassMeta
310         })
311         Map map = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map) eType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap();
312         var keyType = eType.getKeyType() != null ? eType.getKeyType() : string();
313         var valueType = eType.getValueType() != null ? eType.getValueType() : object();
314         for (var line : dataLines) {
315            var cells = splitTableRow(line);
316            if (cells.size() < 2)
317               continue;
318            var rawKey = cells.get(0);
319            var key = parseCellValue(rawKey, keyType, null);
320            // Pass the map being built as the outer when parsing values - non-static inner
321            // classes whose enclosing class is the map type (e.g. H1 inside H extends LinkedHashMap)
322            // need that map instance to satisfy their implicit outer-class constructor argument.
323            var val = parseCellValue(cells.get(1), valueType, map);
324            try {
325               setName(valueType, val, key);
326            } catch (Exception e) {
327               throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not set @NameProperty for map key ''{0}''.", key);
328            }
329            map.put(key, val);
330         }
331         return map;
332      }
333
334      // Object or unknown: return MarshalledMap, check for _type and cast if possible
335      var resultMap = newGenericMap();
336      for (var line : dataLines) {
337         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
338         if (cells.size() < 2)
339            continue;
340         var key = cells.get(0);
341         var val = parseCellValue(cells.get(1), object(), null);
342         resultMap.put(key, val);
343      }
344      
345      // Check for _type property and cast to typed bean if found
346      if (resultMap.containsKey(getBeanTypePropertyName(eType))) {
347         return cast(resultMap, null, eType);
348      }
349      
350      return resultMap;
351   }
352
353   /**
354    * Parses a multi-column Markdown table (N columns).
355    *
356    * @param headers The column header names.
357    * @param dataLines The data row lines.
358    * @param eType The expected type.
359    * @param outer The outer object.
360    * @return The parsed list of beans, maps, or other objects.
361    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
362    */
363   protected Object parseMultiColumnTable(List<String> headers, List<String> dataLines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
364      ClassMeta<?> elementType;
365      Collection<Object> result;
366
367      if (eType.isArray()) {
368         elementType = eType.getElementType();
369         result = new ArrayList<>();
370      } else if (eType.isCollection()) {
371         elementType = eType.getElementType();
372         result = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Collection<Object>) eType.newInstance() : new ArrayList<>();
373      } else if (eType.isObject()) {
374         elementType = object();
375         result = new ArrayList<>();
376      } else {
377         // Single bean/map with multiple columns — not a standard use case, parse first row
378         if (!dataLines.isEmpty()) {
379            var cells = splitTableRow(dataLines.get(0));
380            return parseRow(headers, cells, eType, outer);
381         }
382         return null;
383      }
384
385      for (var line : dataLines) {
386         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
387         var item = parseRow(headers, cells, elementType, result);
388         result.add(item);
389      }
390
391      if (eType.isArray())
392         return toArray(eType, result);
393
394      return result;
395   }
396
397   /**
398    * Parses a single data row into the specified element type.
399    *
400    * @param headers The column header names.
401    * @param cells The cell values for this row.
402    * @param eType The element type.
403    * @param outer The outer object.
404    * @return The parsed element.
405    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
406    */
407   protected Object parseRow(List<String> headers, List<String> cells, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
408      // Check if all cells are null - if so, return null for the entire row
409      boolean allNull = true;
410      for (var cell : cells) {
411         if (cell != null && !cell.equals(nullValue) && !cell.trim().isEmpty()) {
412            allNull = false;
413            break;
414         }
415      }
416      if (allNull) {
417         return null;
418      }
419
420      // Check if there's a _type column and resolve the actual type using the bean registry
421      ClassMeta<?> actualType = eType;
422      int typeColIndex = headers.indexOf(CONST_type);
423      if (typeColIndex >= 0 && typeColIndex < cells.size()) {
424         var typeName = cells.get(typeColIndex);
425         if (ine(typeName)) {
426            var registry = eType != null ? eType.getBeanRegistry() : null;
427            var resolved = registry != null ? registry.getClassMeta(typeName) : null;
428            if (resolved != null)
429               actualType = resolved;
430         }
431      }
432
433      // For types with ObjectSwaps or that can't be populated via BeanMap property-setting,
434      // convert the row to a JSON5 object and delegate to the full JSON5 parser.  This correctly
435      // handles ObjectSwaps, @ParentProperty, JsonMap-constructor beans, etc.
436      if (needsJson5Path(actualType)) {
437         var json5 = rowToJson5(headers, cells);
438            try {
439            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(json5, actualType, outer);
440         } catch (Exception e) {
441            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse table row as ''{0}'' via JSON5 ''{1}''.", actualType, json5);
442         }
443      }
444
445      if (actualType == null || actualType.isObject()) {
446         var m = newGenericMap();
447         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
448            var header = headers.get(i);
449            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
450               continue;
451            var val = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
452            m.put(header, parseCellValue(val, object(), null));
453         }
454         if (typeColIndex >= 0 && m.size() > 0)
455            return cast(m, null, eType);
456         return m;
457      }
458      if (actualType.isBean()) {
459         var m = newBeanMap(outer, actualType.inner());
460         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
461            var header = headers.get(i);
462            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
463               continue;
464            var rawVal = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
465            var pm = m.getPropertyMeta(header);
466            if (pm != null) {
467               setCurrentProperty(pm);
468               var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, (ClassMeta<?>) pm.getBeanInfo(), m.getBean(false));
469               pm.set(m, header, val);
470               setCurrentProperty(null);
471            } else {
472               onUnknownProperty(header, m, rawVal);
473            }
474         }
475         return m.getBean();
476      }
477      if (actualType.isMap()) {
478         @SuppressWarnings({
479            "java:S3740" // Raw Map needed for generic map construction from ClassMeta
480         })
481         Map map = actualType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Map) actualType.newInstance(outer) : newGenericMap();
482         var keyType = actualType.getKeyType() != null ? actualType.getKeyType() : string();
483         var valueType = actualType.getValueType() != null ? actualType.getValueType() : object();
484         for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
485            var header = headers.get(i);
486            if (CONST_type.equals(header))
487               continue;
488            var key = convertAttrToType(map, header, keyType);
489            var rawVal = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
490            // Pass the map being built as the outer when parsing values - mirrors the
491            // key-value-table branch so non-static inner classes whose enclosing class is the
492            // map type can be instantiated correctly.
493            var val = parseCellValue(rawVal, valueType, map);
494            map.put(key, val);
495         }
496         return map;
497      }
498      // Simple type: use first cell
499      return parseCellValue(cells.isEmpty() ? null : cells.get(0), actualType, null);
500   }
501
502   /**
503    * Parses a Markdown bulleted list into a collection or array.
504    *
505    * @param lines All input lines.
506    * @param eType The expected type.
507    * @param outer The outer object.
508    * @return The parsed collection, array, or single element.
509    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
510    */
511   protected Object parseBulletList(List<String> lines, ClassMeta<?> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
512      var items = new ArrayList<String>();
513      for (var line : lines) {
514         var trimmed = line.trim();
515         if (trimmed.startsWith("- ") || trimmed.startsWith("* ") || trimmed.startsWith("+ "))
516            items.add(trimmed.substring(2));
517         else if (trimmed.equals("-") || trimmed.equals("*") || trimmed.equals("+"))
518            items.add("");
519      }
520
521      ClassMeta<?> elementType;
522      Collection<Object> result;
523
524      if (eType.isArray()) {
525         elementType = eType.getElementType();
526         result = new ArrayList<>();
527      } else if (eType.isCollection()) {
528         elementType = eType.getElementType();
529         result = eType.canCreateNewInstance(outer) ? (Collection<Object>) eType.newInstance() : new ArrayList<>();
530      } else {
531         elementType = eType.isObject() ? object() : eType;
532         result = new ArrayList<>();
533      }
534
535      for (var item : items)
536         result.add(parseCellValue(item, elementType, null));
537
538      if (eType.isArray())
539         return toArray(eType, result);
540
541      return result;
542   }
543
544   /**
545    * Returns a Json5Parser that shares this session's bean context (swaps, implClasses, dictionary).
546    *
547    * <p>
548    * Memoized via {@link Memoizer} — first call constructs the parser; subsequent calls return the cached instance.
549    */
550   private JsonParser getJson5Parser() {
551      return json5Parser.get();
552   }
553
554   /**
555    * Converts a raw Markdown table cell string to the target type.
556    *
557    * <p>
558    * If the cell matches the configured null value, returns {@code null}.
559    * If the cell is backtick-wrapped JSON5, parses via {@link Json5Parser}.
560    * Otherwise, attempts numeric/boolean auto-detection when the target type is {@code Object}.
561    *
562    * @param <T> The target type.
563    * @param val The raw cell string (already unescaped).
564    * @param eType The target type.
565    * @param outer The outer object (parent bean) for @ParentProperty support.
566    * @return The parsed value.
567    * @throws ParseException If parsing fails.
568    */
569   protected <T> T parseCellValue(String val, ClassMeta<T> eType, Object outer) throws ParseException {
570      if (val == null)
571         return null;
572      val = val.trim();
573      if (nullValue != null && val.equals(nullValue))
574         return null;
575      if (isTrimStrings())
576         val = val.trim();
577      // For String types, preserve empty cells as empty strings; for other types, treat as null
578      if (val.isEmpty())
579         return eType.isString() ? (T) "" : null;
580
581      // Inline JSON5 wrapped in backticks — use context-aware parser with outer for full support
582      // (handles ObjectSwaps, @ParentProperty, JsonMap constructors, etc.)
583      if (val.startsWith("`") && val.endsWith("`") && val.length() > 1) {
584         var inner = val.substring(1, val.length() - 1);
585         try {
586            return getJson5Parser().parseWithOuter(inner, eType, outer);
587         } catch (Exception e) {
588            throw new ParseException(this, e, "Could not parse inline JSON5 ''{0}'' to type ''{1}''.", inner, eType);
589         }
590      }
591
592      // Unescape table cell escaping
593      val = unescapeCell(val);
594
595      if (eType.isObject()) {
596         // Auto-detect type
597         if ("true".equals(val)) return (T) Boolean.TRUE;
598         if ("false".equals(val)) return (T) Boolean.FALSE;
599         try { return (T) Integer.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not int, try next */ }
600         try { return (T) Long.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not long, try next */ }
601         try { return (T) Double.valueOf(val); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not double, treat as string */ }
602         return (T) val;
603      }
604
605      // Date / time / duration cluster: route through the format-aware parsers so the configured
606      // MarshallingContext.get<Format>() hint reaches the wire-form coercion.  Without this the
607      // fall-through to convertToType(val, eType) below would route a bare numeric cell through a
608      // generic Number → T coercion (e.g. Duration.ofMillis(long)) that drops the format hint.
609      if (eType.isDate())
610         return parseDate(val, eType);
611      if (eType.isCalendar())
612         return parseCalendar(val, eType);
613      if (eType.isTemporal())
614         return parseTemporal(val, eType);
615      if (eType.isDuration())
616         return (T) parseDuration(val);
617      if (eType.isPeriod())
618         return (T) parsePeriod(val);
619
620      try {
621         return convertToType(val, eType);
622      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception e) {
623         throw new ParseException(this, "Could not convert Markdown cell value ''{0}'' to type ''{1}''.", val, eType);
624      }
625   }
626
627   /**
628    * Returns true if the given ClassMeta requires the full JSON5 parsing machinery to be correctly
629    * instantiated — i.e., it has an ObjectSwap, cannot be created as a standard bean, or cannot be
630    * populated via direct property-setting through a BeanMap.
631    *
632    * <p>
633    * When this returns true, table rows and key-value tables should be converted to a JSON5 string
634    * and parsed via {@link Json5Parser} (using
635    * {@link Parser#parseWithOuter}) to correctly handle swaps,
636    * {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty}, and non-standard constructors.
637    */
638   private boolean needsJson5Path(ClassMeta<?> type) {
639      if (type == null || type.isObject() || type.isMap() || type.isPrimitive() || type.isNumber() || type.isString())
640         return false;
641      // If the type has an ObjectSwap, it cannot be parsed via BeanMap property-setting
642      if (type.getSwap(this) != null)
643         return true;
644      // If not a bean (no public properties), can't use BeanMap approach
645      return !type.isBean();
646   }
647
648   /**
649    * Converts a single Markdown table cell value to its JSON5 literal representation.
650    *
651    * <p>
652    * Inline JSON5 (backtick-wrapped) is unwrapped directly. Booleans and numbers are written bare.
653    * Everything else is single-quoted as a JSON5 string.
654    */
655   private String cellToJson5(String cell) {
656      if (cell == null || cell.equals(nullValue))
657         return "null";
658      cell = cell.trim();
659      if (cell.isEmpty())
660         return "''";
661      // Inline JSON5 wrapped in backticks — unwrap and embed directly
662      if (cell.startsWith("`") && cell.endsWith("`") && cell.length() > 1)
663         return cell.substring(1, cell.length() - 1);
664      // Unescape Markdown table escaping first
665      cell = unescapeCell(cell);
666      // Booleans and numbers don't need quoting
667      if ("true".equals(cell) || "false".equals(cell))
668         return cell;
669      try { Integer.parseInt(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not int, try next */ }
670      try { Long.parseLong(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not long, try next */ }
671      try { Double.parseDouble(cell); return cell; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* not double, quote as string */ }
672      // String: single-quote with escaping for JSON5
673      return "'" + cell.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("'", "\\'") + "'";
674   }
675
676   /**
677    * Converts a table row (headers + cells) into a JSON5 object string, suitable for parsing via
678    * {@link Json5Parser}.
679    *
680    * <p>
681    * Example: headers=["f1","f2"], cells=["a","2"] → <code>{f1:'a',f2:2}</code>
682    *
683    * <p>
684    * The {@code _type} column, if present, is included as-is (as a quoted string) so that the JSON5
685    * parser can perform type resolution via the bean dictionary.
686    */
687   private String rowToJson5(List<String> headers, List<String> cells) {
688      var sb = new StringBuilder("{");
689      var first = true;
690      for (var i = 0; i < headers.size(); i++) {
691         var header = headers.get(i);
692         var cell = i < cells.size() ? cells.get(i) : null;
693         if (!first) sb.append(",");
694         first = false;
695         sb.append(header).append(":");
696         if (CONST_type.equals(header)) {
697            // Keep _type as a quoted string so the JSON5 parser can resolve it
698            sb.append(cell == null ? "null" : "'" + cell + "'");
699         } else {
700            sb.append(cellToJson5(cell));
701         }
702      }
703      sb.append("}");
704      return sb.toString();
705   }
706
707   /**
708    * Converts a key-value table (list of [key, value] row lines) into a JSON5 object string.
709    */
710   private String keyValueTableToJson5(List<String> dataLines) {
711      var sb = new StringBuilder("{");
712      var first = true;
713      for (var line : dataLines) {
714         var cells = splitTableRow(line);
715         if (cells.size() < 2)
716            continue;
717         var key = cells.get(0);
718         var val = cells.get(1);
719         if (!first) sb.append(",");
720         first = false;
721         sb.append(key).append(":");
722         if (CONST_type.equals(key)) {
723            sb.append(val == null ? "null" : "'" + val + "'");
724         } else {
725            sb.append(cellToJson5(val));
726         }
727      }
728      sb.append("}");
729      return sb.toString();
730   }
731
732   /**
733    * Splits a Markdown table row into its cell values, stripping leading/trailing whitespace and pipes.
734    *
735    * @param line The raw table row line.
736    * @return The list of trimmed cell values.
737    */
738   protected List<String> splitTableRow(String line) {
739      var trimmed = line.trim();
740      if (trimmed.startsWith("|"))
741         trimmed = trimmed.substring(1);
742      if (trimmed.endsWith("|") && (trimmed.length() < 2 || trimmed.charAt(trimmed.length() - 2) != '\\'))
743         trimmed = trimmed.substring(0, trimmed.length() - 1);
744
745      // Split on unescaped pipe characters
746      var result = new ArrayList<String>();
747      var sb = new StringBuilder();
748      var i = 0;
749      while (i < trimmed.length()) {
750         var c = trimmed.charAt(i);
751         if (c == '\\' && i + 1 < trimmed.length()) {
752            var next = trimmed.charAt(i + 1);
753            if (next == '|' || next == '\\') {
754               sb.append('\\');
755               sb.append(next);
756               i += 2;
757               continue;
758            }
759         }
760         if (c == '|') {
761            result.add(sb.toString().trim());
762            sb.setLength(0);
763         } else {
764            sb.append(c);
765         }
766         i++;
767      }
768      if (!sb.isEmpty() || !result.isEmpty())
769         result.add(sb.toString().trim());
770      return result;
771   }
772
773   /**
774    * Returns whether a table row is a separator row (e.g. {@code |---|---|}).
775    *
776    * @param line The table row line.
777    * @return Whether it is a separator row.
778    */
779   protected boolean isSeparatorRow(String line) {
780      var trimmed = line.trim();
781      if (!trimmed.startsWith("|"))
782         return false;
783      // Equivalent of trimmed.replaceAll("[|\\-: ]", "").isEmpty(): true iff every char is one of | - : space.
784      for (var i = 0; i < trimmed.length(); i++) {
785         var c = trimmed.charAt(i);
786         if (c != '|' && c != '-' && c != ':' && c != ' ')
787            return false;
788      }
789      return true;
790   }
791
792   /**
793    * Reads all lines from the given reader.
794    *
795    * @param r The reader.
796    * @return All lines.
797    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
798    */
799   protected List<String> readAllLines(BufferedReader r) throws IOException {
800      var lines = new ArrayList<String>();
801      String line;
802      while ((line = r.readLine()) != null)
803         lines.add(line);
804      return lines;
805   }
806
807   /**
808    * Unescapes Markdown table cell escape sequences.
809    *
810    * @param s The escaped cell value.
811    * @return The unescaped value.
812    */
813   protected String unescapeCell(String s) {
814      if (s == null || !s.contains("\\"))
815         return s;
816      var sb = new StringBuilder(s.length());
817      var i = 0;
818      while (i < s.length()) {
819         var c = s.charAt(i);
820         if (c == '\\' && i + 1 < s.length()) {
821            var next = s.charAt(i + 1);
822            if (next == '|' || next == '\\') {
823               sb.append(next);
824               i += 2;
825               continue;
826            }
827         }
828         sb.append(c);
829         i++;
830      }
831      return sb.toString();
832   }
833}
